intro: Arthrochilium giganteum, also known as the Giant Arthrochilium, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion. The bioactive constituents of Arthrochilium giganteum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Arthrochilium giganteum is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and syrups to treat respiratory infections and other ailments.
